data.title: implementation-plan skill does not create RED test tasks for skill/prompt file changes
data.description:
## Summary
When /dso:implementation-plan generates tasks that modify skill SKILL.md files or reviewer prompt files, it exempts them from RED test tasks under the static assets TDD exemption (criterion 3). However, skill changes have a testing pattern used elsewhere in the project — skill evals via promptfoo (run-skill-evals.sh). The implementation-plan skill should create RED eval tasks for skill/prompt changes using the same pattern, rather than blanket-exempting them as static assets.

## Reproduction
1. Run /dso:implementation-plan on an epic that modifies SKILL.md or reviewer prompt files
2. Observe that tasks for those files claim TDD exemption: "Static assets only (criterion 3)"
3. No RED test task is created despite skill evals being the established testing mechanism

## Expected Behavior
When a task modifies a skill SKILL.md or reviewer prompt file, the implementation-plan should create a RED eval task that adds or modifies a promptfoo eval scenario asserting the new behavior, following the same pattern as run-skill-evals.sh and generate-skill-eval.sh.

## Relevant Files
- plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md (Step 3 Directives, TDD Task Structure, Unit Test Exemption Criteria)
- plugins/dso/scripts/run-skill-evals.sh
- plugins/dso/scripts/generate-skill-eval.sh
- plugins/dso/docs/SKILL-EVALS-GUIDE.md
